title: Worth It
description: A full day in the mountains, cold, cloudy, and quiet. Then this little guy showed up on the way down. Worth it.
longDescription:
It had been a long day. Cold, overcast, and mostly silent. I had hiked deep into the mountains with the camera and come up largely empty. The kind of day where you start negotiating with yourself about whether the effort was worth it.

On the way back down the trail I caught a flicker of movement and stopped. I set the tripod down, slowed my breathing, and started listening. Then they appeared. A small mixed flock working the rocks and trees right beside me. A brown creeper, a chickadee, and this attractive little pair of red-breasted nuthatches.

My first ever.

In birding, seeing a species for the first time is called a lifer. For a wildlife photographer, it means seeing something new and having your camera in your hand when it happens. I had both. This little male landed on a lichen covered rock, looked around like he owned the mountain, and gave me exactly what I needed.

I photograph wildlife the way I believe it should be done. No bait, no calls, no disturbance. Just patience, a good lens, and the willingness to slow down when the day tells you to.

No nuthatches were bothered in the making of this photograph.
tools: Tools: Sony a1 II & Sony FE 600mm f/4 GM OSS
